summaryText: Guest speaker Tauren Wells opened by expressing his deep, enduring love for the church, a love that began at age nine when he first felt a sense of belonging and gave his life to Christ. He acknowledged the 'shadow side' of church, sharing personal experiences of being hurt, including when members left his congregation over his interracial marriage. Despite these wounds, he emphasized that the church is God's dream and the living, breathing bride of Christ—not a political platform, museum, or showroom for the perfect, but a hospital for the broken and a conduit of God's grace. The sermon, anchored in Ephesians 2:19-22, taught that our lives must be built on the foundational truth of Christ and the apostles, not on shifting cultural or political sands, to withstand life's storms. Pastor Wells challenged the congregation to move beyond individualism and personal preference, to be connected to both the Head (Christ) and the Body (the church) to fulfill their God-given purpose. He illustrated this with a guitar string analogy, showing that tension within the body is necessary to produce a beautiful sound and that our purpose is discovered in serving something greater than ourselves. He concluded with a call to consistent, priority-driven attendance and investment in the local church, urging believers to see it as the place where we encounter God's presence, become more like Jesus, and connect to our divine purpose.
mainPoints: The church is the foundational, living bride of Christ designed for belonging and purpose, not a surface-level institution.
A genuine love for the church requires moving past personal hurt, preference, and individualism to be connected to Christ and His body.
Our God-given purpose and spiritual growth are fulfilled through consistent, sacrificial investment and participation in the local church community.

versesMentioned.text: Consequently, you are no longer foreigners and strangers, but fellow citizens with God’s people and also members of his household, built on the foundation of the apostles and prophets, with Christ Jesus himself as the chief cornerstone. In him the whole building is joined together and rises to become a holy temple in the Lord. And in him you too are being built together to become a dwelling in which God lives by his Spirit.
He went to Nazareth, where he had been brought up, and on the Sabbath day he went into the synagogue, as was his custom. He stood up to read,

Monday.scripture: Ephesians 2:19-20 - '...you are...members of his household, built on the foundation of the apostles and prophets, with Christ Jesus himself as the chief cornerstone.'
Monday.action: Identify one area of your life (e.g., a relationship, career decision) where you've been building on a cultural or personal preference. Pray about how to re-align it with the foundational truth of Christ.
Monday.prayer: Lord, show me where my life is built on sand and help me rebuild it today on the solid rock of Your word and truth.

Tuesday.scripture: Hebrews 10:24-25 - 'And let us consider how we may spur one another on toward love and good deeds, not giving up meeting together...'
Tuesday.action: Intentionally plan and commit to attending your next church service. If you usually attend, reach out to encourage someone else to join you.
Tuesday.prayer: Jesus, give me a fresh hunger for Your house. Help me prioritize gathering with Your people and see it as a non-negotiable part of my week.

Wednesday.scripture: 1 Corinthians 12:27 - 'Now you are the body of Christ, and each one of you is a part of it.'
Wednesday.action: Reflect on your spiritual gifts. Take one step to connect that gift to serving in your church community, whether through a team, a ministry need, or encouraging a leader.
Wednesday.prayer: Holy Spirit, show me my place in the Body. Connect my gifts to the needs of Your church so I can operate in the purpose You designed for me.

Thursday.scripture: Luke 4:16 - '...on the Sabbath day he went into the synagogue, as was his custom.'
Thursday.action: Evaluate your weekly rhythm. Make one practical adjustment to ensure church attendance becomes a 'custom' or healthy habit for you and your household.
Thursday.prayer: God, give me the discipline and desire to follow the example of Jesus, who made gathering with Your people His regular practice.

Friday.scripture: Ephesians 4:16 - 'From him the whole body, joined and held together by every supporting ligament, grows and builds itself up in love, as each part does its work.'
Friday.action: Identify a 'tension' you feel about your church (e.g., a need you see). Instead of criticizing, propose one constructive, small step you could take to be part of the solution.
Friday.prayer: Father, help me see tension not as a reason to disconnect, but as Your invitation to grow, serve, and contribute to the health of Your body.

Saturday.scripture: Psalm 122:1 - 'I rejoiced with those who said to me, “Let us go to the house of the Lord.”'
Saturday.action: Prepare your heart for worship tomorrow. Write down one reason you are thankful for your church and share it with a family member or friend.
Saturday.prayer: Lord, restore my joy and excitement for Your church. I choose to lay down past hurts and anticipate encountering You anew with my church family tomorrow.
